name: ODE-Based Neuroendocrine Dynamics with Hypothalamic Integration
description: Continuous-time ODE model of gut hormone kinetics (CCK, GLP-1, PYY, ghrelin) discretized per simulation tick, with a hypothalamic feeding-drive integrator that sums orexigenic and anorexigenic signals to produce a net hunger drive. The agent eats when the feeding drive is positive and food is available, otherwise navigates toward food or stays put. Based on neuroendocrine models from Yoo & Park (2021), Berthoud (2011), and PMC11483575 (2024).

variables.name: cck_level
variables.description: Plasma cholecystokinin concentration — short-acting satiety signal released by fat/protein intake

variables.name: glp1_level
variables.description: Plasma GLP-1 concentration — medium-acting satiety signal from ileal/colonic L-cells

variables.name: pyy_level
variables.description: Plasma Peptide YY concentration — longer-acting satiety signal proportional to caloric load

variables.name: ghrelin_level
variables.description: Plasma ghrelin concentration — orexigenic hunger signal that rises during fasting

variables.name: stomach_fill
variables.description: Gastric nutrient content representing recent food intake (0=empty, 1=full)

variables.name: feeding_drive
variables.description: Net hypothalamic drive to eat (positive=hungry, negative=sated). Integrates all hormonal signals.

variables.name: energy_reserve
variables.description: Internal energy store analogous to adiposity / leptin-signaled reserve

variables.name: ate_last_tick
variables.description: Binary indicator: did the agent successfully eat on the previous tick

parameters.name: cck_secretion_rate
parameters.source: Berthoud (2011): CCK is rapidly released by fat/protein

parameters.name: glp1_secretion_rate
parameters.source: Yoo & Park (2021): GLP-1 rises proportionally to caloric load

parameters.name: pyy_secretion_rate
parameters.source: PMC11483575 (2024): PYY release proportional to calories

parameters.name: cck_clearance_rate
parameters.source: Berthoud (2011): CCK has shortest half-life among satiety peptides

parameters.name: glp1_clearance_rate
parameters.source: Yoo & Park (2021): GLP-1 half-life ~2 min, sustained by DPP-4 dynamics

parameters.name: pyy_clearance_rate
parameters.source: PMC11483575 (2024): PYY has longer half-life than CCK

parameters.name: ghrelin_rise_rate
parameters.source: MDPI Nutrients (2023): ghrelin rises tonically during fasting

parameters.name: movement_energy_cost
parameters.source: Metabolic cost of movement/basal metabolism per tick

parameters.name: eating_energy_gain
parameters.source: Energy from consuming one food item

rules.description: CCK kinetics: rapid secretion on eating, fast clearance
rules.pseudocode:
H_CCK = H_CCK + k_sec_CCK * ate_last_tick - k_cl_CCK * H_CCK
H_CCK = max(H_CCK, 0.0)

rules.description: GLP-1 kinetics: secretion depends on stomach fill and eating, medium clearance
rules.pseudocode:
H_GLP1 = H_GLP1 + k_sec_GLP1 * S * ate_last_tick - k_cl_GLP1 * H_GLP1
H_GLP1 = max(H_GLP1, 0.0)

rules.description: PYY kinetics: secretion proportional to stomach fill, slow clearance
rules.pseudocode:
H_PYY = H_PYY + k_sec_PYY * S - k_cl_PYY * H_PYY
H_PYY = max(H_PYY, 0.0)

rules.description: Ghrelin dynamics: rises during fasting, suppressed by eating
rules.pseudocode:
G = G + k_G_rise * (1.0 - S) - k_G_sup * ate_last_tick * G
G = max(G, 0.0)

rules.description: Stomach fill: decays via gastric emptying, increased by eating
rules.pseudocode:
S = S - delta * S + ate_last_tick * (1.0 - S) * 0.5
S = max(0.0, min(1.0, S))

rules.description: Energy reserve: depletes with basal cost, replenished by eating
rules.pseudocode:
E = E - E_cost + E_gain * ate_last_tick
E = max(E, 0.0)

rules.description: Hypothalamic feeding drive: net orexigenic minus anorexigenic signals plus energy deficit correction
rules.pseudocode: F = alpha * G - beta1 * H_CCK - beta2 * H_GLP1 - beta3 * H_PYY + gamma * (E_set - E)

decision_logic.description: Agent decides based on the sign and magnitude of the hypothalamic feeding drive F. When hungry (F>0), it eats if food is at current position, otherwise moves toward nearest food. When sated (F<=0), it stays put.
pseudocode: # 1. Update internal state using rules R1-R7
apply_rules(R1, R2, R3, R4, R5, R6, R7)
# 2. Check if food is at agent's current position
food_here = any(f for f in perception.resources.food if f.x == perception.x and f.y == perception.y)
# Find nearest food by Manhattan distance
nearest = min(perception.resources.food, key=lambda f: abs(f.x - perception.x) + abs(f.y - perception.y))
# Move in the direction that reduces Manhattan distance most
return Action('move_right') if dx > 0 else Action('move_left')
return Action('move_down') if dy > 0 else Action('move_up')

perception_to_variables.ate_last_tick: 1 if last_action was 'eat' and reward > 0 else 0

env_mapping.reward_source: eat action → ConsumeEffect reward of 1.0 when food is at agent position

expected_behaviors.description: Ghrelin rises during fasting, increasing feeding drive
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: run 50 steps without food nearby → assert G increases monotonically and F increases over time

expected_behaviors.description: Satiety peptides spike after eating, temporarily suppressing feeding drive
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: agent eats food → assert H_CCK, H_GLP1, H_PYY all increase next tick → assert F decreases below pre-meal level

expected_behaviors.description: CCK decays fastest, PYY decays slowest among satiety peptides
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: after a single meal, track decay → assert H_CCK returns to baseline before H_GLP1, which returns before H_PYY

expected_behaviors.description: Agent stays put when fully sated (F <= 0)
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: after multiple meals in quick succession → assert agent chooses 'stay' for several ticks until F > 0

expected_behaviors.description: Energy depletion increases feeding urgency even when stomach is partially full
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: set E far below E_set → assert F is elevated due to gamma * (E_set - E) term

references: Berthoud, H.R. (2011) — The role of the gut/brain axis in modulating food intake — Neuropharmacology
Yoo, J.Y. & Park, S. (2021) — Role of the gut-brain axis in energy and glucose metabolism — npj Science of Food / Nature
MDPI Nutrients (2023) — The Mechanism of the Gut-Brain Axis in Regulating Food Intake — Nutrients 15(17):3728 (PubMed 37686760)
Pharmacology Research & Perspectives (2024) — The gut-brain axis in appetite, satiety, food intake, and eating disorders — PMC11483575
